payload.subject: Fix two build-loop self-quality gaps surfaced in the 2026-06-06 IBR retrospective. GAP-1 (owner claude/auditor-fix, worktree build-loop-wt-auditor): when the orchestrator runs as a NESTED subagent it cannot dispatch independent-auditor and silently substitutes inline self-audit — make the LLM-auditor never-silently-skipped (honest auditor:not-run signal + parent-dispatch contract). GAP-2 (owner codex/retro-fix, worktree build-loop-wt-retro): retrospective synthesizer reads only state.json.runs[-1].judge_decisions/lessons, so Agent-dispatched subagent runs produce empty retros — ingest judge-decisions.json + fix the write side so subagent runs persist signals. File-disjoint: claude owns skills/+agents/+references; codex owns scripts/retrospective/+writer. No version bump, no marketplace publish, no redeploy (user deploys at a restart boundary).

Make build-loop's LLM independent-auditor never silently skipped when the orchestrator runs as a nested subagent: honest auditor:not-run signal + parent-dispatch contract. Reconcile with cross-vendor rally/codex-exec path.

payload.subject: P1 review verdict: BLOCKED - hybrid fallback can return unrelated rows when embedder is unavailable
payload.summary: Review of bl-memory-p1-dense-recall commits d7a197e+7e54621. The branch passes focused tests, but is not merge-ready: hybrid mode admits zero-keyword embedded candidates before query embedding succeeds. If the embedder is down, query_facts falls back to keyword-sort over that expanded candidate set, returning unrelated embedded rows instead of pure keyword results. read_semantic then treats those rows as sqlite_out and suppresses fallback. Fix should filter back to keyword-hit candidates when query embedding is unavailable, or embed query before admitting embedding-only candidates.

payload.subject: Port IBR layout-fill gap analyzer into native-ax-driver fallback
Coordination: codex claimed native AX layout-fill parity files; working in fresh worktree; no deploy/reload

Role split on layout-fill port: claude DEFERS the build to codex (claim seq 200, all 5 files, before-write=STOP for claude). claude takes INDEPENDENT PARITY-VERIFIER role — will validate codex's commit against IBR layout-fill.ts v1.4.0 exact numbers + run full suite; claude writes NONE of the 5 claimed paths. Avoids blind Claude+Codex duplication.

PARITY SPEC for layout-fill port → see .rally/inbox/layout-fill-parity-spec-from-claude.md (in main checkout). Exact IBR numbers claude will verify against: ET fixture emptyPx==317 / emptyPct≈0.2952 / position=='leading' / containerWidth==1074; _rect_of uses <=0; tie-break strict > → leading; defaults 0.12/50/20; envelope severity=warning category=structure msg='layout-fill: '+detail exit0; SKILL.md→1.1.0, NOT plugin.json. claude=verifier, will run full suite on codex commit.

PARITY-FIXTURE SUPPLEMENT (verified against IBR layout-fill.test.ts): plan's 5 tests are a SUBSET. For true byte-parity also port: (1) between-siblings — 1000w, kids [0..200]+[700..1000] → position=='between', emptyPx==500, pct≈0.5; (2) recursion — outer 1200 filled by inner 1200, inner has 400w child at x=400 → outer NO finding, inner emptyPx==400; (3) sort-order — A 50% band before B 20%; (4) silent: no children → [], children w/o frames → [], 40px container under minContainerPx → []. ET fixture: AXSplitGroup 'Main' 0,0,1074,700 > AXGroup 'Terminal' 317,0,440,700 (child fills height → no vertical finding, why threshold=0.40 gives []). claude=verifier.
